summary: Typosquat of @juggle/resize-observer ships cross-platform crypto-clipper with persistence
details: Package name @jaggle/resizeobserves is a typosquat of the popular @juggle/resize-observer polyfill (README is copied verbatim from the legitimate package, including imports `from '@juggle/resize-observer'`), but ships no ResizeObserver code. On install, npm-install.cjs escalates privileges via pkexec/sudo to apt-get/pacman/dnf-install xclip/wl-clipboard, fetches https://bootstrap.pypa.io/get-pip.py to /tmp and runs `python3 get-pip.py --user` if pip is missing, then `pip install --break-system-packages` of the bundled `clipboard_guardian` Python package (bypassing PEP 668). The Python daemon is then registered as a deceptively-named persistent service on all three OS families: a systemd user unit `python3-dbus-helper.service` (with `loginctl enable-linger` so it runs without login), a macOS LaunchAgent `com.apple.python.runtime.plist`, and a Windows Scheduled Task `PyRuntimeBroker` — names chosen to impersonate OS components. The daemon (clipboard_guardian/guardian.py) polls the clipboard every 0.4s, regex-matches wallet addresses across ~40 cryptocurrencies (BTC/ETH/SOL/XMR/TRX/TON/ADA/XRP/...), and silently rewrites the clipboard to attacker-controlled wallets hardcoded in npm-install.cjs and guardian.py (e.g., ETH 0x450c0E58Fc2ba03632d3F5780ad8C966648B6F18, BTC bc1qs2mpls..., XMR 42zhAidVhP7QETk...). The process renames itself to match the impersonated service names and idles when system monitors (htop, taskmgr, Activity Monitor) are open — anti-forensics confirming malicious intent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
